---
title: Dashboards
description: Charts, metrics, and widgets answering "how is work flowing?" — declared in YAML, live-updated.
---

Dashboards go beyond boards by showing *aggregate* views — throughput, cycle time, SLA breach rate, approval bottlenecks, workload by assignee. Like every other config primitive, they're YAML files in your repo.

## File location

`.kiket/dashboards/*.yaml`. One file per dashboard. Addressable at `/dashboards/<key>` in the app.

## Minimum dashboard

```yaml
key: team-health
name: Team health

widgets:
  - kind: stat
    title: Open issues
    query: "state_category == 'active'"

  - kind: line-chart
    title: Throughput (last 30 days)
    metric: issues.completed
    group_by: day

  - kind: bar-chart
    title: Average cycle time by type
    metric: cycle_time.p50
    group_by: issueType
```

## Widget kinds

| Kind | Shows |
|------|-------|
| `stat` | Single number (count, p50, sum) |
| `line-chart` | Time series |
| `bar-chart` | Grouped counts |
| `pie-chart` | Share breakdown |
| `heatmap` | Calendar heatmap (days × assignees) |
| `table` | Tabular rows with sortable columns |
| `issue-list` | Filtered list of issues |
| `sla-stoplight` | Green/amber/red across workflows |
| `approval-queue` | Who owes an approval right now |

## Metrics

Built-in metrics:

- `issues.created` / `issues.completed`
- `cycle_time.mean|p50|p95` — time in active states
- `lead_time.mean|p50|p95` — time from creation to completion
- `sla.breach_rate`
- `approvals.pending_count` / `approvals.latency.mean`
- `ai.suggestion_acceptance_rate`

Custom metrics:

```yaml
widgets:
  - kind: stat
    title: Contract value this quarter
    query: 'workflow == "contract_review" && completed_at >= start_of_quarter'
    aggregate: sum(fields.total_value_usd)
```

## Filters

Global dashboard filters are surfaced as dropdowns at the top:

```yaml
filters:
  - kind: date-range
    default: last_30_days
  - kind: workflow
  - kind: assignee
```

Every widget respects the active filters.

## Sharing

Dashboards can be made **public** with a password or a signed URL — for exec reviews without giving stakeholders full app access:

```yaml
sharing:
  mode: public
  password: "***"
  allow_export: true
```

Public dashboards are read-only and have no sidebar.
